name: Zoni (Rice Cake Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the chicken very thin and sprinkle with cornstarch.
2. Pound the chicken with the back of a knife to enlarge the pieces.
3. Boil in water for 5 minutes.
4. Slice the Kamaboko into 6 pieces, 1/4 inch thick.
5. Slice the carrot into thin slices and peel the taro, then slice them into thin round slices.
6. Boil the carrots and taro in 1/2 cup of dashi.
7. When soft, add 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon shoyu.
8. Boil the spinach or watercress in hot water, ensuring they are still very green when removed.
9. Cut the greens into 1-inch lengths.
10. Slice the Yuzu into thin strips to represent pine needles.
11. Toast the rice cakes.
12. Boil 6 cups of dashi and flavor with 1 tablespoon salt and 1 tablespoon shoyu.
13. Add the Kamaboko, carrots, taro, and greens to each of six bowls.
14. Place two pieces of toasted Mochi, two slices of chicken, 1 slice of carrot, 2 slices of taro, 1 slice of kamaboko, and a little of the greens in each bowl.
15. Pour the hot soup over them and float the Yuzu needles on top.
16. Traditionally, drink TOSO wine before eating Zoni, starting with the master of the house, followed by family members and servants.
17. Serve Fuku-cha (good fortune tea) with pickled plum in tiny cups to protect against illness during the coming year.
